rulings.Case Title: MARY ANN MEADOWS BALL et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Co-Defendant Schneider Electric Usa, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action (Forum Non-Conveniens)
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 3, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. DEFENDANT ITT CORPORATION FKA ITT INDUSTRIES, INC.'s Joinder To Co-Defendant Schneider Electric Usa, Inc.'s Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action (Forum Non-Conveniens). Hearing required. Anyone who contends that the case would not be time-barred if filed in Alabama should be prepared to explain why. The court will inquire whether all defendants will waive the benefit of the statute of limitations in Alabama. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA LAZARUS et al VS. A.B. BOYD CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Crane Co.S Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 3, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5. DEFENDANT SEPCO CORPORATION's Joinder In Defendant Crane Co¿s Motion to dismiss for forum-non-conveniens is denied. Crane Co. has not shown that all named defendants are subject to jurisdiction in Oregon. DEFENDANT SEPCO CORPORATION's Joinder In Defendant Crane Co¿s alternate motion to stay is granted. Given the decedent¿s lengthy alleged exposure history in Oregon and minimal California contacts, the public and private factors identified in Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744 weigh in favor of this case being litigated in Oregon. The stay is conditioned upon a finding by an Oregon court that every named defendant is subject to jurisdiction in that state. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD R. WILMOT et al VS. ACE HARDWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 3, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. Defendant American Biltrite¿s demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant¿s demurrer based on the statute of limitations is overruled. A statute of limitations bar does not appear from the face of the complaint and plaintiff adequately alleges that his ¿disability within the meaning of California Code of Civil Procedure section 340.2 either has yet to occur or occurred less than a year before filing of this complaint and thus this complaint is timely under law.¿ Defendant¿s demurrer based on improper venue is overruled. Improper venue is not a cognizable ground for demurrer. C.C.P. §430.10. Defendant¿s demurrer to the negligence and strict liability claims for failure to state a cause of action and for being uncertain is overruled. Plaintiffs plead sufficient facts to satisfy the standard set forth in Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 80 by identifying ¿square vinyl tiles,¿ ¿asphalt tiles,¿ ¿joint compound¿ and various automotive parts as the asbestos-containing products. Defendant¿s demurrer to the breach of implied warranty claim for failing to state a cause of action and for being uncertain is overruled. Claims for breach of implied warranty have not been superseded by the law of strict liability. (See Carlin v. Superior Court (1996) 13 Cal.4th 1104, 1117-1118). Defendant¿s demurrer to the fraud/failure to warn claim for failing to state a cause of action and for being uncertain is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to fraud and overruled as to failure to warn. As to fraud, plaintiffs failed to allege ¿the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written.¿ (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157). As to failure to warn, plaintiffs adequately alleged that defendant

rulings.Case Title: GERALD R. WILMOT et al VS. ACE HARDWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 3, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. Defendant American Biltrite¿s Motion to strike is granted in part and denied in part. Page 3, ¶7, lines 23-28 through page 4 lines 1-2 is stricken. Plaintiffs have not alleged the facts that serve as the basis for his claim that ¿each of the Defendants¿was the agent, servant, employee and/or joint venturer, of his co-defendant.¿ The request to strike the portions of the complaint dealing with punitive damages is denied. Plaintiffs have stated a claim for punitive damages by alleging that defendant was aware of the hazards of asbestos and nevertheless sold asbestos-containing products while intentionally concealing the information about hazards from Mr. Wilmot and the public. = (220/HEK)
